NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. Do not keep silent, O God of my praise!
NKJV.2: For the mouth of the wicked and the mouth of the deceitful Have opened against me; They have spoken against me with a lying tongue.
NKJV.3: They have also surrounded me with words of hatred, And fought against me without a cause.
NKJV.4: In return for my love they are my accusers, But I give myself to prayer.
NKJV.5: Thus they have rewarded me evil for good, And hatred for my love.
NKJV.6: Set a wicked man over him, And let an accuser stand at his right hand.
NKJV.7: When he is judged, let him be found guilty, And let his prayer become sin.
NKJV.8: Let his days be few, And let another take his office.
NKJV.9: Let his children be fatherless, And his wife a widow.
NKJV.10: Let his children continually be vagabonds, and beg; Let them seek their bread also from their desolate places.
NKJV.11: Let the creditor seize all that he has, And let strangers plunder his labor.
NKJV.12: Let there be none to extend mercy to him, Nor let there be any to favor his fatherless children.
NKJV.13: Let his posterity be cut off, And in the generation following let their name be blotted out.
NKJV.14: Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before the LORD, And let not the sin of his mother be blotted out.
NKJV.15: Let them be continually before the LORD, That He may cut off the memory of them from the earth;
NKJV.16: Because he did not remember to show mercy, But persecuted the poor and needy man, That he might even slay the broken in heart.
NKJV.17: As he loved cursing, so let it come to him; As he did not delight in blessing, so let it be far from him.
NKJV.18: As he clothed himself with cursing as with his garment, So let it enter his body like water, And like oil into his bones.
NKJV.19: Let it be to him like the garment which covers him, And for a belt with which he girds himself continually.
NKJV.20: Let this be the LORD’s reward to my accusers, And to those who speak evil against my person.
NKJV.21: But You, O GOD the Lord, Deal with me for Your name’s sake; Because Your mercy is good, deliver me.
NKJV.22: For I am poor and needy, And my heart is wounded within me.
NKJV.23: I am gone like a shadow when it lengthens; I am shaken off like a locust.
NKJV.24: My knees are weak through fasting, And my flesh is feeble from lack of fatness.
NKJV.25: I also have become a reproach to them; When they look at me, they shake their heads.
NKJV.26: Help me, O LORD my God! Oh, save me according to Your mercy,
NKJV.27: That they may know that this is Your hand— That You, LORD, have done it!
NKJV.28: Let them curse, but You bless; When they arise, let them be ashamed, But let Your servant rejoice.
NKJV.29: Let my accusers be clothed with shame, And let them cover themselves with their own disgrace as with a mantle.
NKJV.30: I will greatly praise the LORD with my mouth; Yes, I will praise Him among the multitude.
NKJV.31: For He shall stand at the right hand of the poor, To save him from those who condemn him.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. Hold not thy peace<S>2790</S>, O God<S>430</S> of my praise<S>8416</S>;
KJV.2: For the mouth<S>6310</S> of the wicked<S>7563</S> and the mouth<S>6310</S> of the deceitful<S>4820</S> are opened<S>6605</S> against me: they have spoken<S>1696</S> against me with a lying<S>8267</S> tongue<S>3956</S>. <sup>of the deceitful: Heb. of deceit</sup> <sup>are opened: Heb. have opened themselves</sup>
KJV.3: They compassed<S>5437</S> me about also with words<S>1697</S> of hatred<S>8135</S>; and fought<S>3898</S> against me without a cause<S>2600</S>.
KJV.4: For my love<S>160</S> they are my adversaries<S>7853</S>: but I give myself unto prayer<S>8605</S>.
KJV.5: And they have rewarded<S>7760</S> me evil<S>7451</S> for good<S>2896</S>, and hatred<S>8135</S> for my love<S>160</S>.
KJV.6: Set<S>6485</S> thou a wicked man<S>7563</S> over him: and let Satan<S>7854</S> stand<S>5975</S> at his right hand<S>3225</S>. <sup>Satan: or, an adversary</sup>
KJV.7: When he shall be judged<S>8199</S>, let him be<S>3318</S> condemned<S>7563</S>: and let his prayer<S>8605</S> become sin<S>2401</S>. <sup>be condemned: Heb. go out guilty, or, wicked</sup>
KJV.8: Let his days<S>3117</S> be few<S>4592</S>; and let another<S>312</S> take<S>3947</S> his office<S>6486</S>. <sup>office: or, charge</sup>
KJV.9: Let his children<S>1121</S> be fatherless<S>3490</S>, and his wife<S>802</S> a widow<S>490</S>.
KJV.10: Let his children<S>1121</S> be continually<S>5128</S> vagabonds<S>5128</S>, and beg<S>7592</S>: let them seek<S>1875</S> their bread also out of their desolate places<S>2723</S>.
KJV.11: Let the extortioner<S>5383</S> catch<S>5367</S> all that he hath; and let the strangers<S>2114</S> spoil<S>962</S> his labour<S>3018</S>.
KJV.12: Let there be none to extend<S>4900</S> mercy<S>2617</S> unto him: neither let there be any to favour<S>2603</S> his fatherless children<S>3490</S>.
KJV.13: Let his posterity<S>319</S> be cut off<S>3772</S>; and in the generation<S>1755</S> following<S>312</S> let their name<S>8034</S> be blotted out<S>4229</S>.
KJV.14: Let the iniquity<S>5771</S> of his fathers<S>1</S> be remembered<S>2142</S> with the LORD<S>3068</S>; and let not the sin<S>2403</S> of his mother<S>517</S> be blotted out<S>4229</S>.
KJV.15: Let them be before the LORD<S>3068</S> continually<S>8548</S>, that he may cut off<S>3772</S> the memory<S>2143</S> of them from the earth<S>776</S>.
KJV.16: Because that he remembered<S>2142</S> not to shew<S>6213</S> mercy<S>2617</S>, but persecuted<S>7291</S> the poor<S>6041</S> and needy<S>34</S> man<S>376</S>, that he might even slay<S>4191</S> the broken<S>3512</S> in heart<S>3824</S>.
KJV.17: As he loved<S>157</S> cursing<S>7045</S>, so let it come<S>935</S> unto him: as he delighted<S>2654</S> not in blessing<S>1293</S>, so let it be far<S>7368</S> from him.
KJV.18: As he clothed<S>3847</S> himself with cursing<S>7045</S> like as with his garment<S>4055</S>, so let it come<S>935</S> into his bowels<S>7130</S> like water<S>4325</S>, and like oil<S>8081</S> into his bones<S>6106</S>. <sup>into his bowels: Heb. within him</sup>
KJV.19: Let it be unto him as the garment<S>899</S> which covereth<S>5844</S> him, and for a girdle<S>4206</S> wherewith he is girded<S>2296</S> continually<S>8548</S>.

KJV.22: For I am poor<S>6041</S> and needy<S>34</S>, and my heart<S>3820</S> is wounded<S>2490</S> within<S>7130</S> me.
KJV.23: I am gone<S>1980</S> like the shadow<S>6738</S> when it declineth<S>5186</S>: I am tossed up and down<S>5287</S> as the locust<S>697</S>.
KJV.24: My knees<S>1290</S> are weak<S>3782</S> through fasting<S>6685</S>; and my flesh<S>1320</S> faileth<S>3584</S> of fatness<S>8081</S>.

KJV.30: I will greatly<S>3966</S> praise<S>3034</S> the LORD<S>3068</S> with my mouth<S>6310</S>; yea, I will praise<S>1984</S> him among<S>8432</S> the multitude<S>7227</S>.
KJV.31: For he shall stand<S>5975</S> at the right hand<S>3225</S> of the poor<S>34</S>, to save<S>3467</S> him from those that condemn<S>8199</S> his soul<S>5315</S>. <sup>those: Heb. the judges of</sup>

ESV.1: Be not silent, O God of my praise!
ESV.2: For wicked and deceitful mouths are opened against me, speaking against me with lying tongues.
ESV.3: They encircle me with words of hate, and attack me without cause.
ESV.4: In return for my love they accuse me, but I give myself to prayer.
ESV.5: So they reward me evil for good, and hatred for my love.
ESV.6: Appoint a wicked man against him; let an accuser stand at his right hand.
ESV.7: When he is tried, let him come forth guilty; let his prayer be counted as sin!
ESV.8: May his days be few; may another take his office!
ESV.9: May his children be fatherless and his wife a widow!
ESV.10: May his children wander about and beg, seeking food far from the ruins they inhabit!
ESV.11: May the creditor seize all that he has; may strangers plunder the fruits of his toil!
ESV.12: Let there be none to extend kindness to him, nor any to pity his fatherless children!
ESV.13: May his posterity be cut off; may his name be blotted out in the second generation!
ESV.14: May the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before the LORD, and let not the sin of his mother be blotted out!
ESV.15: Let them be before the LORD continually, that he may cut off the memory of them from the earth!
ESV.16: For he did not remember to show kindness, but pursued the poor and needy and the brokenhearted, to put them to death.
ESV.17: He loved to curse; let curses come  upon him! He did not delight in blessing; may it be far  from him!
ESV.18: He clothed himself with cursing as his coat; may it soak  into his body like water, like oil into his bones!
ESV.19: May it be like a garment that he wraps around him, like a belt that he puts on every day!
ESV.20: May this be the reward of my accusers from the LORD, of those who speak evil against my life!
ESV.21: But you, O GOD my Lord, deal on my behalf for your name's sake; because your steadfast love is good, deliver me!
ESV.22: For I am poor and needy, and my heart is stricken within me.
ESV.23: I am gone like a shadow at evening; I am shaken off like a locust.
ESV.24: My knees are weak through fasting; my body has become gaunt, with no fat.
ESV.25: I am an object of scorn to my accusers; when they see me, they wag their heads.
ESV.26: Help me, O LORD my God! Save me according to your steadfast love!
ESV.27: Let them know that this is your hand; you, O LORD, have done it!
ESV.28: Let them curse, but you will bless! They arise and are put to shame, but your servant will be glad!
ESV.29: May my accusers be clothed with dishonor; may they be wrapped in their own shame as in a cloak!
ESV.30: With my mouth I will give great thanks to the LORD; I will praise him in the midst of the throng.
ESV.31: For he stands at the right hand of the needy one, to save him from those who condemn his soul to death.

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of David. O God of my praise, Do not be silent!
NASB.2: For they have opened the wicked and deceitful mouth against me; They have spoken against me with a lying tongue.
NASB.3: They have also surrounded me with words of hatred, And fought against me without cause.
NASB.4: In return for my love they act as my accusers; But I am [in] prayer.
NASB.5: Thus they have repaid me evil for good And hatred for my love.
NASB.6: Appoint a wicked man over him, And let an accuser stand at his right hand.
NASB.7: When he is judged, let him come forth guilty, And let his prayer become sin.
NASB.8: Let his days be few; Let another take his office.
NASB.9: Let his children be fatherless And his wife a widow.
NASB.10: Let his children wander about and beg; And let them seek [sustenance] far from their ruined homes.
NASB.11: Let the creditor seize all that he has, And let strangers plunder the product of his labor.
NASB.12: Let there be none to extend lovingkindness to him, Nor any to be gracious to his fatherless children.
NASB.13: Let his posterity be cut off; In a following generation let their name be blotted out.
NASB.14: Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before the LORD, And do not let the sin of his mother be blotted out.
NASB.15: Let them be before the LORD continually, That He may cut off their memory from the earth;
NASB.16: Because he did not remember to show lovingkindness, But persecuted the afflicted and needy man, And the despondent in heart, to put [them] to death.
NASB.17: He also loved cursing, so it came to him; And he did not delight in blessing, so it was far from him.
NASB.18: But he clothed himself with cursing as with his garment, And it entered into his body like water And like oil into his bones.
NASB.19: Let it be to him as a garment with which he covers himself, And for a belt with which he constantly girds himself.
NASB.20: Let this be the reward of my accusers from the LORD, And of those who speak evil against my soul.
NASB.21: But You, O GOD, the Lord, deal [kindly] with me for Your name's sake; Because Your lovingkindness is good, deliver me;
NASB.22: For I am afflicted and needy, And my heart is wounded within me.
NASB.23: I am passing like a shadow when it lengthens; I am shaken off like the locust.
NASB.24: My knees are weak from fasting, And my flesh has grown lean, without fatness.
NASB.25: I also have become a reproach to them; When they see me, they wag their head.
NASB.26: Help me, O LORD my God; Save me according to Your lovingkindness.
NASB.27: And let them know that this is Your hand; You, LORD, have done it.
NASB.28: Let them curse, but You bless; When they arise, they shall be ashamed, But Your servant shall be glad.
NASB.29: Let my accusers be clothed with dishonor, And let them cover themselves with their own shame as with a robe.
NASB.30: With my mouth I will give thanks abundantly to the LORD; And in the midst of many I will praise Him.
NASB.31: For He stands at the right hand of the needy, To save him from those who judge his soul.

NLT.1:
O God, whom I praise,
don’t stand silent and aloof
NLT.2:
while the wicked slander me
and tell lies about me.
NLT.3:
They surround me with hateful words
and fight against me for no reason.
NLT.4:
I love them, but they try to destroy me with accusations
even as I am praying for them!
NLT.5:
They repay evil for good,
and hatred for my love.
NLT.6:
They say, “Get an evil person to turn against him.
Send an accuser to bring him to trial.
NLT.7:
When his case comes up for judgment,
let him be pronounced guilty.
Count his prayers as sins.
NLT.8:
Let his years be few;
let someone else take his position.
NLT.9:
May his children become fatherless,
and his wife a widow.
NLT.10:
May his children wander as beggars
and be driven from their ruined homes.
NLT.11:
May creditors seize his entire estate,
and strangers take all he has earned.
NLT.12:
Let no one be kind to him;
let no one pity his fatherless children.
NLT.13:
May all his offspring die.
May his family name be blotted out in the next generation.
NLT.14:
May the LORD never forget the sins of his fathers;
may his mother’s sins never be erased from the record.
NLT.15:
May the LORD always remember these sins,
and may his name disappear from human memory.
NLT.16:
For he refused all kindness to others;
he persecuted the poor and needy,
and he hounded the brokenhearted to death.
NLT.17:
He loved to curse others;
now you curse him.
He never blessed others;
now don’t you bless him.
NLT.18:
Cursing is as natural to him as his clothing,
or the water he drinks,
or the rich food he eats.
NLT.19:
Now may his curses return and cling to him like clothing;
may they be tied around him like a belt.”
NLT.20:
May those curses become the LORD’s punishment
for my accusers who speak evil of me.
NLT.21:
But deal well with me, O Sovereign LORD,
for the sake of your own reputation!
Rescue me
because you are so faithful and good.
NLT.22:
For I am poor and needy,
and my heart is full of pain.
NLT.23:
I am fading like a shadow at dusk;
I am brushed off like a locust.
NLT.24:
My knees are weak from fasting,
and I am skin and bones.
NLT.25:
I am a joke to people everywhere;
when they see me, they shake their heads in scorn.
NLT.26:
Help me, O LORD my God!
Save me because of your unfailing love.
NLT.27:
Let them see that this is your doing,
that you yourself have done it, LORD.
NLT.28:
Then let them curse me if they like,
but you will bless me!
When they attack me, they will be disgraced!
But I, your servant, will go right on rejoicing!
NLT.29:
May my accusers be clothed with disgrace;
may their humiliation cover them like a cloak.
NLT.30:
But I will give repeated thanks to the LORD,
praising him to everyone.
NLT.31:
For he stands beside the needy,
ready to save them from those who condemn them.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. God of my praise, don’t remain silent,
WEB.2: for they have opened the mouth of the wicked and the mouth of deceit against me. They have spoken to me with a lying tongue.
WEB.3: They have also surrounded me with words of hatred, and fought against me without a cause.
WEB.4: In return for my love, they are my adversaries; but I am in prayer.
WEB.5: They have rewarded me evil for good, and hatred for my love.
WEB.6: Set a wicked man over him. Let an adversary stand at his right hand.
WEB.7: When he is judged, let him come out guilty. Let his prayer be turned into sin.
WEB.8: Let his days be few. Let another take his office.
WEB.9: Let his children be fatherless, and his wife a widow.
WEB.10: Let his children be wandering beggars. Let them be sought from their ruins.
WEB.11: Let the creditor seize all that he has. Let strangers plunder the fruit of his labor.
WEB.12: Let there be no one to extend kindness to him, neither let there be anyone to have pity on his fatherless children.
WEB.13: Let his posterity be cut off. In the generation following let their name be blotted out.
WEB.14: Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered by Yahweh. Don’t let the sin of his mother be blotted out.
WEB.15: Let them be before Yahweh continually, that he may cut off their memory from the earth;
WEB.16: because he didn’t remember to show kindness, but persecuted the poor and needy man, the broken in heart, to kill them.
WEB.17: Yes, he loved cursing, and it came to him. He didn’t delight in blessing, and it was far from him.
WEB.18: He clothed himself also with cursing as with his garment. It came into his inward parts like water, like oil into his bones.
WEB.19: Let it be to him as the clothing with which he covers himself, for the belt that is always around him.
WEB.20: This is the reward of my adversaries from Yahweh, of those who speak evil against my soul.
WEB.21: But deal with me, Yahweh the Lord,  for your name’s sake, because your loving kindness is good, deliver me;
WEB.22: for I am poor and needy. My heart is wounded within me.
WEB.23: I fade away like an evening shadow. I am shaken off like a locust.
WEB.24: My knees are weak through fasting. My body is thin and lacks fat.
WEB.25: I have also become a reproach to them. When they see me, they shake their head.
WEB.26: Help me, Yahweh, my God. Save me according to your loving kindness;
WEB.27: that they may know that this is your hand; that you, Yahweh, have done it.
WEB.28: They may curse, but you bless. When they arise, they will be shamed, but your servant shall rejoice.
WEB.29: Let my adversaries be clothed with dishonor. Let them cover themselves with their own shame as with a robe.
WEB.30: I will give great thanks to Yahweh with my mouth. Yes, I will praise him among the multitude.
WEB.31: For he will stand at the right hand of the needy, to save him from those who judge his soul.

CSB17.1: For the choir director. A psalm of David. [1] God of my praise, do not be silent. ⓘ
CSB17.2:
For wicked and deceitful mouths open against me;
they speak against me with lying tongues. ⓙ
CSB17.3:
They surround me with hateful words
and attack me without cause. ⓚ
CSB17.4:
In return for my love they accuse me,
but I continue to pray. [1] ⓛ
CSB17.5:
They repay me evil for good,
and hatred for my love. ⓜ
CSB17.6:
Set a wicked person over him;
let an accuser [2] stand at his right hand. ⓝ
CSB17.7:
When he is judged, let him be found guilty,
and let his prayer be counted as sin. ⓐ
CSB17.8:
Let his days be few;
let another take over his position. ⓑ
CSB17.9:
Let his children be fatherless
and his wife a widow. ⓒ
CSB17.10:
Let his children wander as beggars,
searching for food far [3] from their demolished homes. ⓓ
CSB17.11:
Let a creditor seize all he has;
let strangers plunder what he has worked for. ⓔ
CSB17.12:
Let no one show him kindness,
and let no one be gracious to his fatherless children. ⓕ
CSB17.13:
Let the line of his descendants be cut off;
let their name be blotted out in the next generation. ⓖ
CSB17.14:
Let the iniquity of his fathers
be remembered before the LORD, ⓗ
and do not let his mother’s sin be blotted out. ⓘ
CSB17.15:
Let their sins [4] always remain before the LORD,
and let him remove [5] all memory of them from the earth. ⓙ
CSB17.16:
For he did not think to show kindness,
but pursued the suffering, needy, and brokenhearted
in order to put them to death. ⓚ
CSB17.17:
He loved cursing — let it fall on him;
he took no delight in blessing — let it be far from him. ⓛ
CSB17.18:
He wore cursing like his coat —
let it enter his body like water
and go into his bones like oil. ⓜ
CSB17.19:
Let it be like a robe he wraps around himself,
like a belt he always wears. ⓝ
CSB17.20:
Let this be the LORD’s payment to my accusers,
to those who speak evil against me. ⓐ
CSB17.21:
But you, LORD, my Lord,
deal kindly with me for your name’s sake; ⓑ
because your faithful love is good, rescue me. ⓒ
CSB17.22:
For I am suffering and needy; ⓓ
my heart is wounded within me. ⓔ
CSB17.23:
I fade away like a lengthening shadow; ⓕ
I am shaken off like a locust.
CSB17.24:
My knees are weak from fasting,
and my body is emaciated. [6] ⓖ
CSB17.25:
I have become an object of ridicule to my accusers; [7]
when they see me, they shake their heads in scorn. ⓗ
CSB17.26:
Help me, LORD my God;
save me according to your faithful love ⓘ
CSB17.27:
so they may know that this is your hand
and that you, LORD, have done it. ⓙ
CSB17.28:
Though they curse, you will bless.
When they rise up, they will be put to shame,
but your servant will rejoice. ⓚ
CSB17.29:
My accusers will be clothed with disgrace;
they will wear their shame like a cloak. ⓛ
CSB17.30:
I will fervently thank the LORD with my mouth;
I will praise him in the presence of many. ⓜ
CSB17.31:
For he stands at the right hand of the needy
to save him from those who would condemn him. ⓝ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. O God of my praise!
Do not keep silent,
AMP.2:
For the mouth of the wicked and the mouth of the deceitful are opened against me;
They have spoken against me with a lying tongue.
AMP.3:
They have also surrounded me with words of hatred,
And have fought against me without a cause.
AMP.4:
In return for my love, they attack me,
But I am in prayer.
AMP.5:
They have repaid me evil for good,
And hatred for my love.
AMP.6:
Appoint a wicked man against him,
And let an attacker stand at his right hand [to kill him].
AMP.7:
When he enters into dispute, let wickedness come about.
Let his prayer [for help] result [only] in sin.
AMP.8:
Let his days be few;
And let another take his office.
AMP.9:
Let his children be fatherless
And his wife a widow.
AMP.10:
Let his children wander and beg;
Let them seek their food and be driven far from their ruined homes.
AMP.11:
Let the creditor seize all that he has,
And let strangers plunder the product of his labor.
AMP.12:
Let there be no one to extend kindness to him,
Nor let anyone be gracious to his fatherless children.
AMP.13:
Let his descendants be cut off,
And in the following generation let their name be blotted out.
AMP.14:
Let the wickedness of his fathers be remembered by the LORD;
And do not let the sin of his mother be blotted out.
AMP.15:
Let them be before the LORD continually,
That He may cut off their memory from the earth;
AMP.16:
Because the man did not remember to show kindness,
But persecuted the suffering and needy man,
And the brokenhearted, to put them to death.
AMP.17:
He also loved cursing, and it came [back] to him;
He did not delight in blessing, so it was far from him.
AMP.18:
He clothed himself with cursing as with his garment,
And it seeped into his inner self like water
And like [anointing] oil into his bones.
AMP.19:
Let it be to him as a robe with which he covers himself,
And as a sash with which he is constantly bound.
AMP.20:
Let this be the reward of my attackers from the LORD,
And of those who speak evil against my life.
AMP.21:
But You, O GOD, the Lord, show kindness to me, for Your name's sake;
Because Your lovingkindness (faithfulness, compassion) is good, O rescue me;
AMP.22:
For I am suffering and needy,
And my heart is wounded within me.
AMP.23:
I am vanishing like a shadow when it lengthens and fades;
I am shaken off like the locust.
AMP.24:
My knees are unsteady from fasting;
And my flesh is gaunt and without fatness.
AMP.25:
I also have become a reproach and an object of taunting to others;
When they see me, they shake their heads [in derision].
AMP.26:
Help me, O LORD my God;
Save me according to Your lovingkindness—
AMP.27:
And let them know that this is Your hand;
You, LORD, have done it.
AMP.28:
Let them curse, but You bless.
When adversaries arise, let them be ashamed,
But let Your servant rejoice.
AMP.29:
Let my attackers be clothed with dishonor,
And let them cover themselves with their own shame as with a robe.
AMP.30:
I will give great praise and thanks to the LORD with my mouth;
And in the midst of many I will praise Him.
AMP.31:
For He will stand at the right hand of the needy,
To save him from those who judge his soul.

MSG.1:
A David prayer
My God, don't turn a deaf ear to my hallelujah prayer.
MSG.2:
Liars are pouring out invective on me;
Their lying tongues are like a pack of dogs out to get me,
MSG.3: barking their hate, nipping my heels—and for no reason!
MSG.4:
I loved them and now they slander me—yes, me!—
and treat my prayer like a crime;
MSG.5:
They return my good with evil,
they return my love with hate.
MSG.6:
Send the Evil One to accuse my accusing judge;
dispatch Satan to prosecute him.
MSG.7:
When he's judged, let the verdict be, "Guilty,"
and when he prays, let his prayer turn to sin.
MSG.8:
Give him a short life,
and give his job to somebody else.
MSG.9:
Make orphans of his children,
dress his wife in widow's weeds;
MSG.10:
Turn his children into begging street urchins,
evicted from their homes—homeless.
MSG.11:
May the bank foreclose and wipe him out,
and strangers, like vultures, pick him clean.
MSG.12:
May there be no one around to help him out,
no one willing to give his orphans a break.
MSG.13:
Chop down his family tree
so that nobody even remembers his name.
MSG.14:
But erect a memorial to the sin of his father,
and make sure his mother's name is there, too—
MSG.15:
Their sins recorded forever before God,
but they themselves sunk in oblivion.
MSG.16:
That's all he deserves since he was never once kind,
hounded the afflicted and heartbroken to their graves.
MSG.17:
Since he loved cursing so much,
let curses rain down;
Since he had no taste for blessing,
let blessings flee far from him.
MSG.18:
He dressed up in curses like a fine suit of clothes;
he drank curses, took his baths in curses.
MSG.19:
So give him a gift—a costume of curses;
he can wear curses every day of the week!
MSG.20:
That's what they'll get, those out to get me—
an avalanche of just deserts from God.
MSG.21:
Oh, God, my Lord, step in;
work a miracle for me—you can do it!
Get me out of here—your love is so great!—
MSG.22: I'm at the end of my rope, my life in ruins.
MSG.23:
I'm fading away to nothing, passing away,
my youth gone, old before my time.
MSG.24:
I'm weak from hunger and can hardly stand up,
my body a rack of skin and bones.
MSG.25:
I'm a joke in poor taste to those who see me;
they take one look and shake their heads.
MSG.26:
Help me, oh help me, God, my God,
save me through your wonderful love;
MSG.27:
Then they'll know that your hand is in this,
that you, God, have been at work.
MSG.28:
Let them curse all they want;
you do the blessing.
MSG.29:
Let them be jeered by the crowd when they stand up,
followed by cheers for me, your servant.
Dress my accusers in clothes dirty with shame,
discarded and humiliating old ragbag clothes.
MSG.30:
My mouth's full of great praise for God,
I'm singing his hallelujahs surrounded by crowds,
MSG.31:
For he's always at hand to take the side of the needy,
to rescue a life from the unjust judge.

NIV.1:
Psalm 109
For the director of music. Of David. A psalm.
O God, whom I praise,
do not remain silent,
NIV.2:
for wicked and deceitful men
have opened their mouths against me;
they have spoken against me with lying tongues.
NIV.3:
With words of hatred they surround me;
they attack me without cause.
NIV.4:
In return for my friendship they accuse me,
but I am a man of prayer.
NIV.5:
They repay me evil for good,
and hatred for my friendship.
NIV.6:
Appoint an evil man to oppose him;
let an accuser stand at his right hand.
NIV.7:
When he is tried, let him be found guilty,
and may his prayers condemn him.
NIV.8:
May his days be few;
may another take his place of leadership.
NIV.9:
May his children be fatherless
and his wife a widow.
NIV.10:
May his children be wandering beggars;
may they be driven from their ruined homes.
NIV.11:
May a creditor seize all he has;
may strangers plunder the fruits of his labor.
NIV.12:
May no one extend kindness to him
or take pity on his fatherless children.
NIV.13:
May his descendants be cut off,
their names blotted out from the next generation.
NIV.14:
May the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before the Lord;
may the sin of his mother never be blotted out.
NIV.15:
May their sins always remain before the Lord,
that he may cut off the memory of them from the earth.
NIV.16:
For he never thought of doing a kindness,
but hounded to death the poor
and the needy and the brokenhearted.
NIV.17:
He loved to pronounce a curse—
may it come on him;
he found no pleasure in blessing—
may it be far from him.
NIV.18:
He wore cursing as his garment;
it entered into his body like water,
into his bones like oil.
NIV.19:
May it be like a cloak wrapped about him,
like a belt tied forever around him.
NIV.20:
May this be the Lord’s payment to my accusers,
to those who speak evil of me.
NIV.21:
But you, O Sovereign Lord,
deal well with me for your name’s sake;
out of the goodness of your love, deliver me.
NIV.22:
For I am poor and needy,
and my heart is wounded within me.
NIV.23:
I fade away like an evening shadow;
I am shaken off like a locust.
NIV.24:
My knees give way from fasting;
my body is thin and gaunt.
NIV.25:
I am an object of scorn to my accusers;
when they see me, they shake their heads.
NIV.26:
Help me, O Lord my God;
save me in accordance with your love.
NIV.27:
Let them know that it is your hand,
that you, O Lord, have done it.
NIV.28:
They may curse, but you will bless;
when they attack they will be put to shame,
but your servant will rejoice.
NIV.29:
My accusers will be clothed with disgrace
and wrapped in shame as in a cloak.
NIV.30:
With my mouth I will greatly extol the Lord;
in the great throng I will praise him.
NIV.31:
For he stands at the right hand of the needy one,
to save his life from those who condemn him.

NRSVCE.1: Do not be silent, O God of my praise.
NRSVCE.2:
For wicked and deceitful mouths are opened against me,
speaking against me with lying tongues.
NRSVCE.3:
They beset me with words of hate,
and attack me without cause.
NRSVCE.4:
In return for my love they accuse me,
even while I make prayer for them.
NRSVCE.5:
So they reward me evil for good,
and hatred for my love.
NRSVCE.6:
They say, “Appoint a wicked man against him;
let an accuser stand on his right.
NRSVCE.7:
When he is tried, let him be found guilty;
let his prayer be counted as sin.
NRSVCE.8:
May his days be few;
may another seize his position.
NRSVCE.9:
May his children be orphans,
and his wife a widow.
NRSVCE.10:
May his children wander about and beg;
may they be driven out of the ruins they inhabit.
NRSVCE.11:
May the creditor seize all that he has;
may strangers plunder the fruits of his toil.
NRSVCE.12:
May there be no one to do him a kindness,
nor anyone to pity his orphaned children.
NRSVCE.13:
May his posterity be cut off;
may his name be blotted out in the second generation.
NRSVCE.14:
May the iniquity of his father be remembered before the LORD,
and do not let the sin of his mother be blotted out.
NRSVCE.15:
Let them be before the LORD continually,
and may his memory be cut off from the earth.
NRSVCE.16:
For he did not remember to show kindness,
but pursued the poor and needy
and the brokenhearted to their death.
NRSVCE.17:
He loved to curse; let curses come on him.
He did not like blessing; may it be far from him.
NRSVCE.18:
He clothed himself with cursing as his coat,
may it soak into his body like water,
like oil into his bones.
NRSVCE.19:
May it be like a garment that he wraps around himself,
like a belt that he wears every day.”
NRSVCE.20:
May that be the reward of my accusers from the LORD,
of those who speak evil against my life.
NRSVCE.21:
But you, O LORD my Lord,
act on my behalf for your name’s sake;
because your steadfast love is good, deliver me.
NRSVCE.22:
For I am poor and needy,
and my heart is pierced within me.
NRSVCE.23:
I am gone like a shadow at evening;
I am shaken off like a locust.
NRSVCE.24:
My knees are weak through fasting;
my body has become gaunt.
NRSVCE.25:
I am an object of scorn to my accusers;
when they see me, they shake their heads.
NRSVCE.26:
Help me, O LORD my God!
Save me according to your steadfast love.
NRSVCE.27:
Let them know that this is your hand;
you, O LORD, have done it.
NRSVCE.28:
Let them curse, but you will bless.
Let my assailants be put to shame; may your servant be glad.
NRSVCE.29:
May my accusers be clothed with dishonor;
may they be wrapped in their own shame as in a mantle.
NRSVCE.30:
With my mouth I will give great thanks to the LORD;
I will praise him in the midst of the throng.
NRSVCE.31:
For he stands at the right hand of the needy,
to save them from those who would condemn them to death.

NET.1: O God whom I praise, do not ignore me!
NET.2: For they say cruel and deceptive things to me; they lie to me.
NET.3: They surround me and say hateful things; they attack me for no reason.
NET.4: They repay my love with accusations, but I continue to pray.
NET.5: They repay me evil for good, and hate for love.
NET.6: Appoint an evil man to testify against him! May an accuser stand at his right side!
NET.7: When he is judged, he will be found guilty! Then his prayer will be regarded as sinful.
NET.8: May his days be few! May another take his job!
NET.9: May his children be fatherless, and his wife a widow!
NET.10: May his children roam around begging, asking for handouts as they leave their ruined home!
NET.11: May the creditor seize all he owns! May strangers loot his property!
NET.12: May no one show him kindness! May no one have compassion on his fatherless children!
NET.13: May his descendants be cut off! May the memory of them be wiped out by the time the next generation arrives!
NET.14: May his ancestors’ sins be remembered by the LORD! May his mother’s sin not be forgotten!
NET.15: May the LORD be constantly aware of them, and cut off the memory of his children from the earth!
NET.16: For he never bothered to show kindness; he harassed the oppressed and needy, and killed the disheartened.
NET.17: He loved to curse others, so those curses have come upon him. He had no desire to bless anyone, so he has experienced no blessings.
NET.18: He made cursing a way of life, so curses poured into his stomach like water and seeped into his bones like oil.
NET.19: May a curse attach itself to him, like a garment one puts on, or a belt one wears continually!
NET.20: May the LORD repay my accusers in this way, those who say evil things about me!
NET.21: O sovereign LORD, intervene on my behalf for the sake of your reputation! Because your loyal love is good, deliver me!
NET.22: For I am oppressed and needy, and my heart beats violently within me.
NET.23: I am fading away like a shadow at the end of the day; I am shaken off like a locust.
NET.24: I am so starved my knees shake; I have turned into skin and bones.
NET.25: I am disdained by them. When they see me, they shake their heads.
NET.26: Help me, O LORD my God! Because you are faithful to me, deliver me!
NET.27: Then they will realize this is your work, and that you, LORD, have accomplished it.
NET.28: They curse, but you will bless. When they attack, they will be humiliated, but your servant will rejoice.
NET.29: My accusers will be covered with shame, and draped in humiliation as if it were a robe.
NET.30: I will thank the LORD profusely, in the middle of a crowd I will praise him,
NET.31: because he stands at the right hand of the needy, to deliver him from those who threaten his life.
